steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize an objective of the GREEN SURGE study. The notes state one primary aim explicitly: to determine the benefits of UNAs in European cities. The correct choice must make that aim the main point of the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "For multiple years, the GREEN SURGE study was conducted in cities throughout Europe.": Describes the study's duration and scope, not its objective. ✗
  • "To achieve its main objective, the GREEN SURGE study examined forty-four different types of UNAs, including large urban parks.": Mentions an objective exists but never states what it is — the reader learns nothing about the actual aim. ✗
  • "Determining the benefits of UNAs in European cities was a primary aim of the GREEN SURGE study.": Makes the study's objective the grammatical subject and central point of the sentence, directly matching the note "A primary aim of the study was to determine the benefits of UNAs in these cities." ✓
  • "Large urban parks, which were included in the GREEN SURGE study, can benefit urban environments by contributing to biodiversity.": Focuses on what urban parks do biologically, not on the study's objective. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
